meta_title: Impact Doors in Lauderdale Manors, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact doors in Lauderdale Manors, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Call for a free quote!
h1: Impact Doors Built for Lauderdale Manors: Protecting Your Home Where It Starts — At the Front Door
intro:
Lauderdale Manors is a close-knit community tucked just west of Fort Lauderdale, bordered by Lauderhill, Oakland Park, and some of Broward County's most established residential streets. It's a neighborhood where families have planted roots, where front porches see real life, and where the average home — valued around $260,000 — represents years of hard work and sacrifice. But like every community in South Florida, Lauderdale Manors sits squarely in the path of the Atlantic hurricane season, and the threats that come with it demand more than a deadbolt and a prayer. That's exactly why more homeowners here are turning to impact doors as a foundational upgrade — not just for storm season, but for year-round security, energy efficiency, and lasting peace of mind.

Located in the 33311 ZIP code and classified within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), Lauderdale Manors is subject to the most rigorous wind load standards in the entire state. With design wind speeds reaching 170 mph in this area, the doors on your home aren't just aesthetic features — they're the first and most critical line of defense when a major storm makes landfall. Standard doors, even those reinforced with hurricane shutters, simply cannot provide the same seamless, code-compliant protection as a purpose-built impact door. And given that roughly 65% of homes in the area were built before 1994 — before modern HVHZ standards were enacted — the need to upgrade entry points is especially urgent throughout this community.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've spent years serving homeowners throughout Broward County, including the streets and neighborhoods that make up Lauderdale Manors. Whether your home backs up near Lauderdale Manors Park or sits along one of the community's quieter residential blocks, we understand the local building requirements, the permit process through the Broward County Building Division, and the specific challenges older South Florida homes present. Impact doors are one of the most valuable investments a Lauderdale Manors homeowner can make — and we're here to make that investment straightforward, affordable, and expertly installed.

section_why.h2: Why Lauderdale Manors Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Doors
section_why.body:
There's no shortage of contractors in Broward County willing to put a door in your frame and call it a day. What sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is a commitment to doing the job right — every time, on every home, in every neighborhood. Known throughout South Florida as "The Kings of Service," our team brings over 60 years of combined industry experience to every project, and every installation is completed entirely by our own in-house crews. We don't subcontract your home to the lowest bidder. We send our trained, licensed professionals who know Lauderdale Manors homes, understand HVHZ requirements, and take pride in results that last.

With over 500 homes completed across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built a reputation that speaks for itself. Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau reflects the way we treat our customers — transparently, respectfully, and with a level of follow-through that's increasingly rare in the home improvement industry. When we walk through your door for a free estimate, we're not just measuring an opening; we're understanding your home, your concerns, and your goals. Then we design a solution that fits all three.

For Lauderdale Manors specifically, our familiarity with the Broward County Building Division permitting process is a major advantage. Impact door installations in the HVHZ require approved product submittals, proper engineering documentation, and thorough inspections — and navigating that process alone can be overwhelming for homeowners. We handle everything from permit application to final inspection sign-off, so you're never left chasing paperwork or wondering about compliance. Our work meets or exceeds every requirement set by the Florida Building Code for HVHZ construction.

We also understand that in a community like Lauderdale Manors — where home values hover around $260,000 and households are budget-conscious — the cost of a major upgrade matters. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it genuinely possible for families to invest in real storm protection without stretching their finances to the breaking point. Quality impact doors shouldn't be reserved only for high-income zip codes, and at Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, they aren't.

section_history.h2: Storms Have Tested Lauderdale Manors Before — and They Will Again
section_history.body:
Lauderdale Manors has faced more than its share of hurricane scares over the past three decades. Since 1990, this area of Broward County has experienced the effects of at least 10 named storms, each one a reminder that South Florida's pleasant weather carries a serious seasonal cost. The most memorable of these storms each left a distinct mark on the community. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 — the catastrophic Category 5 that reshaped Florida's entire approach to building codes — caused widespread structural failures across the region and exposed just how unprepared most residential construction was for extreme wind events. Many homes in Lauderdale Manors that predate 1994 were built to standards that Andrew proved were dangerously inadequate.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought a different kind of chaos — a fast-moving storm with a massive wind field that devastated Broward County communities from coast to inland, knocking out power for weeks and causing extensive damage to roofs, windows, and doors throughout neighborhoods like Lauderdale Manors. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked directly over South Florida and served as yet another stark reminder that no inland community is truly insulated from a major hurricane's reach. Each of these storms generated insurance claims, property damage, and in too many cases, complete loss of home security as compromised entry points allowed wind-driven rain to flood interiors.

The pattern is clear: Lauderdale Manors homeowners cannot afford to rely on outdated doors and standard hardware when storm season arrives. Impact doors, tested and rated for 170 mph wind speeds, are specifically engineered to stand firm under the conditions these historic storms have produced. They don't need to be boarded up, they don't require storm panels to be hauled out of the garage, and they don't leave your home vulnerable during the critical hours before a storm makes landfall. They simply work — storm after storm, year after year.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Standards and Broward County Building Codes: What Lauderdale Manors Homeowners Need to Know
section_code.body:
Lauderdale Manors sits entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone — the HVHZ — a designation that applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties and carries the strictest residential construction requirements in the nation. This isn't a technicality or bureaucratic label; it's a recognition that this region faces extreme wind events that demand extreme engineering standards. Under the Florida Building Code's HVHZ provisions, any impact door installed in Lauderdale Manors must meet specific large-missile and small-missile impact test requirements, along with rigorous cyclic pressure testing that simulates the repeated stress of hurricane-force winds. Only products that have received Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or equivalent approval are legally permitted for installation in HVHZ zones.

The permitting process for impact door installation in Lauderdale Manors runs through the Broward County Building Division. Homeowners are required to pull permits before installation begins, and inspections must be completed at designated stages to confirm compliance with HVHZ specifications. This process exists to protect you — improperly installed or non-compliant doors can void your homeowner's insurance coverage and create significant liability if your home sustains storm damage. Unfortunately, many fly-by-night contractors skip or rush these steps, leaving homeowners exposed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every permit, every submission, and every inspection coordination as a standard part of our service.

For homes built before 1994 — which represents the majority of the housing stock in Lauderdale Manors — upgrading to HVHZ-compliant impact doors may also require assessment of the surrounding framing and structural attachment points. Our in-house installation crews are trained to evaluate these conditions and make any necessary structural preparations before the door is ever hung. This thorough approach ensures that your impact door performs exactly as rated when it matters most, and that every installation passes Broward County inspection the first time.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Built for the HVHZ
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two industry-leading manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands have earned their place in South Florida's most demanding construction market by producing impact-resistant door systems that combine serious engineering with real-world durability and curb appeal. These aren't compromise products — they're built from the ground up to meet and exceed the HVHZ standards that Lauderdale Manors homes require.

ESW impact doors are known for their robust frame construction, multi-point locking systems, and the use of laminated impact glass that holds together even after impact — preventing dangerous glass fragments from becoming projectiles in high-wind conditions. Whether you're looking for a commanding impact entry door to replace a dated front entrance, a sleek sliding glass door for a back patio, or elegant French doors for a living space, ESW offers configurations that deliver both security and style. Their products are tested to withstand 170 mph wind loads, making them a natural fit for the Lauderdale Manors wind zone designation.

CWI brings its own engineering excellence to the lineup, with a strong emphasis on energy efficiency alongside impact protection. In a South Florida climate where air conditioning costs can run year-round, CWI's thermally broken frame designs and low-E glass options deliver meaningful reductions in heat transfer — which translates directly to lower monthly utility bills for Lauderdale Manors homeowners. Both ESW and CWI door systems feature multi-point locking mechanisms that engage at multiple points along the door frame rather than at a single deadbolt, significantly improving both hurricane resistance and everyday home security. When you invest in an impact door from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're choosing a product that works on every level — storm protection, energy savings, and security.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Doors Cost in Lauderdale Manors — and How Do You Finance Them?
section_cost.body:
One of the first questions Lauderdale Manors homeowners ask is: what's this actually going to cost me? It's a fair question, and the honest answer is that impact door pricing varies depending on the type of door — entry, sliding glass, or French — the size, the glass configuration, and any structural preparation required. For a community where the average home value sits around $260,000, a full impact door upgrade represents a meaningful but genuinely worthwhile investment. Entry-level single impact doors typically start in the $1,500–$2,500 installed range, while larger sliding glass door or French door systems can run higher depending on specifications. Custom sizes or configurations found in some of the neighborhood's older homes may affect the final number as well.

The good news is that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes the financial side manageable. We offer financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, allowing homeowners to get HVHZ-compliant protection in place without having to drain savings or delay a critical upgrade. Our team will walk you through available financing programs during your free in-home consultation, and we'll help you understand the total value equation — not just the sticker price, but the long-term return on your investment.

That return is real and measurable. Impact doors increase home resale value, particularly in a market like Broward County where buyers increasingly demand storm-ready homes. They reduce energy costs by improving insulation at one of the home's most common thermal weak points. And perhaps most significantly, they can reduce your annual homeowner's insurance premium by a meaningful percentage — savings that chip away at the total cost of the upgrade year after year. For many Lauderdale Manors homeowners, impact doors effectively pay for themselves over time while providing safety and security that a storm shutter or standard door simply cannot match.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance in Lauderdale Manors
section_insurance.body:
Broward County homeowners are already well acquainted with the financial pain of Florida's homeowner's insurance market. Premiums have risen significantly in recent years as insurers have reassessed their exposure in hurricane-prone regions, and Lauderdale Manors residents — sitting in the HVHZ with a housing stock that skews older — are not immune to that pressure. But here's what many homeowners don't fully realize: the installation of impact-rated doors and windows is one of the most effective actions you can take to reduce your annual insurance costs.

Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer wind mitigation discounts to homeowners who have installed hurricane-resistant opening protection. A licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your impact door installations and submit a wind mitigation report to your insurance company, triggering credits that often amount to hundreds of dollars per year — sometimes more, depending on your carrier and current premium. Common insurers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Heritage, Universal, and others — all recognize HVHZ-approved impact openings as significant risk-reducing features. The credits apply not only to the doors themselves but are enhanced when combined with impact windows protecting all openings throughout the home.

For a Lauderdale Manors homeowner paying elevated premiums on a $260,000 property, these annual savings can be substantial. If your current insurer won't offer meaningful credits, your impact door documentation also becomes a powerful tool when shopping for competitive quotes from other carriers. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is happy to walk you through what to expect from the wind mitigation process and how to maximize the insurance benefit of your impact door investment. We've helped hundreds of Broward County homeowners navigate this process, and the savings our customers report consistently validate the decision to upgrade.

faqs.q: Are impact doors truly necessary for a home in Lauderdale Manors, which is several miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Lauderdale Manors is located in the HVHZ and experiences design wind speeds of 170 mph — regardless of its distance from the coast. Hurricanes like Andrew, Wilma, and Irma caused severe inland damage throughout Broward County. Impact doors are required by the Florida Building Code for all new and replacement door installations in HVHZ areas, and they provide essential protection against wind-driven debris that travels well inland during major storms.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors typically cost in Lauderdale Manors, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Lauderdale Manors vary by door type, size, and configuration, but most projects are very achievable with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's financing options starting at $87 per month with $0 down. During your free in-home estimate, we'll provide a detailed quote and help you explore financing plans that fit your budget. When factoring in energy savings and insurance discounts, the long-term value of impact doors makes them one of the smartest home investments available.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Lauderdale Manors?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door replacements in Lauderdale Manors require permits through the Broward County Building Division. Products must carry Miami-Dade NOA or equivalent HVHZ approval, and installations are subject to inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from application and product documentation to scheduling inspections and securing final approval. You won't have to navigate any of that bureaucracy on your own.

faqs.q: How long does a typical impact door installation take for a Lauderdale Manors home?
faqs.a: Most single-door replacements can be completed in one day by our in-house installation crews. Larger projects involving multiple doors — such as a front entry door combined with sliding glass doors or French doors — may require two days depending on scope and any structural preparation needed for older homes. We'll give you a clear project timeline during your consultation and keep you informed at every step, from permit approval through final inspection.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation credits for impact-rated door installations in HVHZ areas like Lauderdale Manors. After installation, a wind mitigation inspection can document your new doors and trigger discounts from carriers like Citizens, Heritage, Universal, and others. Many Broward County homeowners report saving hundreds of dollars annually — savings that compound over time and help offset the initial investment in impact protection.
